method: read-only official documentation and source-pinned public repository metadata review; no candidate installation or code execution

officialBaselines.finding: Agent Skills is an open folder format with progressive discovery, activation, and execution; it is a portable capability payload format rather than a complete local governance control plane.
The preview MCP Registry is metadata and namespace infrastructure; it delegates code security and expects downstream curation or aggregation.
Tauri provides a Rust-backed cross-platform desktop shell using system webviews, explicit permissions and capabilities, packaging, signing, update, and test surfaces.
Wails provides a Go-backed cross-platform desktop alternative using web frontends and native packaging.

sourcePinnedCandidates.value: multi-agent source parsing, path mapping, add/list/find/update/remove/use workflows
sourcePinnedCandidates.gap: does not supply curated admission, broad capability graph, ownership arbitration, transactional rollback, or runtime observation

sourcePinnedCandidates.value: mature Tauri/Rust evidence across providers, MCP, Skills, and multiple agent environments
sourcePinnedCandidates.gap: foreign owner; cannot become transaction authority without a separate ownership and adapter contract
telemetry and error-reporting dependencies conflict with telemetry-off default until proven removable

sourcePinnedCandidates.value: file watching, local database, Git, and multi-agent UI evidence
sourcePinnedCandidates.gap: heavier Electron footprint and telemetry dependency

sourcePinnedCandidates.value: combined MCP and Skill management validates broader-than-Skills demand
sourcePinnedCandidates.gap: small and comparatively young evidence base

sourcePinnedCandidates.value: Agent Skills package management and native component evidence
sourcePinnedCandidates.gap: does not own local multi-ecosystem policy and transaction governance
not a complete capability governance and rollback control plane

reuseStrategy.preferredIntegration: versioned adapter or subprocess boundary before vendoring or forking
reuseFirst: native host Skill installers when present and verifiably suitable
vercel-labs/skills compatible parsing, source normalization, supported-agent path mapping, and ordinary package operations
mature adjacent manager UX and host-compatibility evidence
official registry metadata as discovery input, never as approval
selfBuildOnlyResidualCore: exact curated release and digest consumption
broad capability graph across native, rules, Skills, Hooks, MCP, Plugins, Apps, tools, services, project mechanisms, and human authority
preview, backup, apply, verify, rollback, lock, and crash-consistent journal
runtime observation, drift evidence, acceptance receipts, and retirement
reuseStrategy.forbiddenShortcut: do not rewrite a generic Skill package manager or treat registry popularity as admission proof

costs: higher Rust learning and iteration cost
platform packaging still requires host-specific CI evidence
weaker direct precedent among reviewed capability managers
desktop permission model needs more application-owned hardening
less suitable as the transaction core

technologyRecommendation.core: Rust library plus CLI using clap, SQLite through rusqlite, and explicit file-system adapter traits
technologyRecommendation.initialClient: CLI plus Ratatui TUI after the transaction core is testable
technologyRecommendation.laterClient: Tauri v2 GUI as a replaceable client over the same core
technologyRecommendation.fallback: Go plus Cobra, Bubble Tea, SQLite, and Wails only if a bounded prototype shows materially better delivery velocity without weakening the security and rollback contract

independentRepositoryAssessment.reason: the residual transaction, migration, security, adapter, local-state, and release authority has a lifecycle distinct from curated content governance and from any one external package manager
independentRepositoryAssessment.retirementCheck: merge or retire if a bounded prototype cannot prove that distinct authority boundary
